result.stdout:
Description:

Usage:
  barista [command] [options]

Options:
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>  The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>      The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  --version                Show version information
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  api-key               Use this command to manage api keys
  api                   Manage apis
  client                Upload, publish and validate clients
  environment           Use this command to manage environments
  launch                This command launches Banana Cake Pop in your default browser
  login                 This command logs you in with a user account. Barista will try to launch a web browser to log you in interactively
  logout                Log out to remove access to Banana Cake Pop
  schema                Upload, publish and validate schemas
  fusion-configuration  Manage fusion configurations
  stage                 Manage stages
  workspace             Use this command to manage workspaces

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:
  barista [command] [options]

Options:
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>  The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>      The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  --version                Show version information
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  api-key               Use this command to manage api keys
  api                   Manage apis
  client                Upload, publish and validate clients
  environment           Use this command to manage environments
  launch                This command launches Banana Cake Pop in your default browser
  login                 This command logs you in with a user account. Barista will try to launch a web browser to log you in interactively
  logout                Log out to remove access to Banana Cake Pop
  schema                Upload, publish and validate schemas
  fusion-configuration  Manage fusion configurations
  stage                 Manage stages
  workspace             Use this command to manage workspaces

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage apis

Usage:
  barista api [command] [options]

Options:
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>  The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>      The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create             Creates a new api
  delete <id>        Deletes a api by id
  list               Lists all apis of a workspace
  show <id>          Shows details of an api
  set-settings <id>  Sets the settings of a api

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upload, publish and validate clients

Usage:
  barista client [command] [options]

Options:
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>  The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>      The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  publish    Publish a client version to an stage
  unpublish  Unpublish a client version from an stage
  validate   Validate a client version
  upload     Upload a new client version
  create     Creates a new client
  delete     Deletes a client
  list       Lists all clients of an api
  show <id>  Shows details of an client
  download   Download the queries from a stage

commands.payload:

  publish    Publish a client version to an stage
  unpublish  Unpublish a client version from an stage

  upload     Upload a new client version
  create     Creates a new client
  delete     Deletes a client
  list       Lists all clients of an api
  show <id>  Shows details of an client
  download   Download the queries from a stage

result.stdout:
Description:
  Download the queries from a stage

Usage:
  barista client download [options]

Options:
  --api-id <api-id> (REQUIRED)  The id of the api [env: BARISTA_API_ID]
  --stage <stage> (REQUIRED)    The name of the stage [env: BARISTA_STAGE]
  --output <output> (REQUIRED)  The path where the client is stored
  --format <folder|relay>       The format of which the client is stored.
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>       The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>           The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  -?, -h, --help                Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Publish a client version to an stage

Usage:
  barista client publish [options]

Options:
  --tag <tag> (REQUIRED)              The tag of the schema version to deploy [env: BARISTA_TAG]
  --stage <stage> (REQUIRED)          The name of the stage [env: BARISTA_STAGE]
  --client-id <client-id> (REQUIRED)  The id of the client [env: BARISTA_CLIENT_ID]
  --force                             The operation will be succeed even when there are errors.
  --wait-for-approval                 Wait for approval [env: BARISTA_SUBGRAPH_NAME] [default: False]
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>             The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>                 The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information

  --api-key <api-key>                 The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  -?, -h, --help                      Show help and usage information

Description:
  Publish a configuration

Usage:
  barista fusion-configuration publish [command] [options]

Options:
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>  The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>      The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  begin     Begin a configuration publish. This command will request a deployment slot
  start     Start a fusion configuration publish.
  validate  Validates a fusion configuration against the schema and clients
  cancel    Cancels a fusion configuration publish.
  commit    Commit a fusion configuration publish.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Begin a configuration publish. This command will request a deployment slot

Usage:
  barista fusion-configuration publish begin [options]

Options:
  --tag <tag> (REQUIRED)           The tag of the schema version to deploy [env: BARISTA_TAG]
  --stage <stage> (REQUIRED)       The name of the stage [env: BARISTA_STAGE]
  --api-id <api-id> (REQUIRED)     The id of the api [env: BARISTA_API_ID]
  --subgraph-id <subgraph-id>      The id of the subgraph [env: BARISTA_SUBGRAPH_ID]
  --subgraph-name <subgraph-name>  The name of the subgraph [env: BARISTA_SUBGRAPH_NAME]
  --wait-for-approval              Wait for approval [env: BARISTA_SUBGRAPH_NAME] [default: False]
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>          The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>              The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Use this command to manage workspaces

Usage:
  barista workspace [command] [options]

Options:
  --cloud-url <cloud-url>  The url of the api. [env: BARISTA_CLOUD_URL] [default: api.chillicream.com]
  --api-key <api-key>      The api key that is used for the authentication [env: BARISTA_API_KEY]
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

Commands:
  create       Creates a new workspace
  current      Shows the name of the currently selected workspace.
  set-default  Use this command to select a workspace and set it as your default workspace
  list         Lists all workspaces
  show <id>    Shows details of a workspace
